001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import static java.util.Collections.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.AnnotationTraversal.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ObjectUtils.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
028import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.eq;
029import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
030
031import java.lang.annotation.*;
032import java.lang.reflect.*;
033import java.net.*;
034import java.util.*;
035import java.util.function.*;
036import java.util.stream.*;
037
038import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
042
043/**
044 * Contains metadata about a bean property.
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * Contains information such as type of property (e.g. field/getter/setter), class type of property value, and whether
048 * any transforms are associated with this property.
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * Developers will typically not need access to this class.  The information provided by it is already exposed through
052 * several methods on the {@link BeanMap} API.
053 *
054 * <h5 class='topic'>Thread safety</h5>
055 *
056 * Instances are immutable after construction and are safe for concurrent read access.
057 *
058 */
059@SuppressWarnings({
060   "rawtypes", // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
061   "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
062   "java:S115",   // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_field, ARG_value)
063   "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - BeanMap<?>, ClassMeta<?> for property metadata
064})
065public class BeanPropertyMeta implements Comparable<BeanPropertyMeta> {
066
067   /**
068    * Sentinel value that, when returned from {@link BeanPropertyMeta.Builder#writeTransform} during
069    * {@link #set(BeanMap, String, Object)}, causes the set to be skipped entirely — neither the configured setter
070    * is invoked nor the property cache updated.
071    *
072    * <p>
073    * Provided as a generic SPI hook so the marshalling layer (or any other transform-installing extension) can
074    * implement "leave the bean's pre-existing value in place" semantics — used by the per-property null-coercion
075    * {@code SKIP} mode added to the marshalling layer in 10.0.0.
076    *
077    * @since 10.0.0
078    */
079   public static final Object SKIP_VALUE = new Object();
080
081   // Property name constants
082   private static final String PROP_field = "field";
083   private static final String PROP_getter = "getter";
084   private static final String PROP_name = "name";
085   private static final String PROP_setter = "setter";
086   private static final String PROP_type = "type";
087
088   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
089   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
090   private static final String ARG_innerField = "innerField";
091
092   // Error message constants
093   private static final String MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined = "Getter or public field not defined on property ''{0}''";
094
095   /**
096    * BeanPropertyMeta builder class.
097    */
098   @SuppressWarnings({
099      "java:S1104" // Public mutable fields are an intentional cross-module SPI seam: the marshalling-side post-processor (different package) reads/writes them after validate(), so they cannot be final or package-private.
100   })
101   public static class Builder {
102      BeanMeta<?> beanMeta;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
103      BeanTypeResolver bc;  // The bean-modeling SPI seam to the marshalling-side type resolver.  Null when the owning BeanMeta was built via the commons-side path.
104      BeanConfigContext config;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access.  Always non-null — sourced from the owning BeanMeta.
105      public String name;  // Public so marshalling-side post-processors can use it when constructing diagnostic messages (e.g. MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor's schema-validation transform).
106      FieldInfo field;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
107      public FieldInfo innerField;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
108      public MethodInfo getter;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
109      public MethodInfo setter;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
110      MethodInfo extraKeys;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
111      public BeanInfo<?> rawTypeMeta;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access (used to install swap-aware transforms).  Null on commons-side path (no type resolution).  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta} since it's the only in-tree implementation; the field is typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam so the field can live in commons.bean.
112      public Object swap;  // Object-typed so the field can live in commons.bean; cast to ObjectSwap by marshalling-side consumers.  Set only via MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor (marshalling-side post-processor).
113      public BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> readTransform;  // Package-private; defaults to identity if null.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam; marshalling-side installers cast the session argument back to {@code MarshallingSession} where needed (see {@code MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor#installSwapAwareTransforms}).
114      public BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> writeTransform; // Package-private; defaults to identity if null.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam (see readTransform note).
115      public List<ClassInfo> dictionaryClasses;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access; @MarshalledProp(dictionary={}) classes scanned during validate().
116      public Set<String> views;  // Named views this property belongs to; null = untagged (in all views when defaultViewInclusion is enabled). Set via MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor.
117      private boolean isConstructorArg;
118      public boolean isUri;  // Package-private so MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor can set @Uri-derived flag.  Mirrors rawTypeMeta.isUri() plus @Uri annotation reads on field/getter/setter.
119      private boolean isDyna;
120      private boolean isDynaGetterMap;
121      public BeanInfo<?> typeMeta;  // Package-private so the marshalling-side post-processor can override after @Swap/@MarshalledProp detection.  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta}; typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam.
122      private Object overrideValue;
123      private BeanPropertyMeta delegateFor;
124      private boolean canRead;
125      private boolean canWrite;
126      private boolean readOnly;
127      private boolean writeOnly;
128
129      Builder(BeanMeta<?> beanMeta, String name) {
130         this.beanMeta = beanMeta;
131         this.bc = beanMeta.getBeanTypeResolver();  // Returns null on commons-side path.
132         this.config = beanMeta.getConfig();
133         this.name = name;
134      }
135
136      /**
137       * @return A new BeanPropertyMeta object using this builder.
138       */
139      public BeanPropertyMeta build() {
140         return new BeanPropertyMeta(this);
141      }
142
143      /**
144       * Sets the original bean property that this one is overriding.
145       *
146       * @param value The original bean property that this one is overriding.
147       * @return This object.
148       */
149      public Builder delegateFor(BeanPropertyMeta value) {
150         delegateFor = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
151         return this;
152      }
153
154      /**
155       * Installs the read-side value transform for this property.
156       *
157       * <p>
158       * Applied to the raw getter result by {@link BeanPropertyMeta#get(BeanMap,String)} before it is returned to the
159       * caller.  Defaults to identity (raw value passes through unchanged).
160       *
161       * <p>
162       * Used by the marshalling layer to install {@code ObjectSwap}-aware behavior at bean-meta construction time;
163       * the bean-modeling layer itself only invokes the function and does not directly reference
164       * {@code ObjectSwap}.
165       *
166       * @param value The transform function.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
167       * @return This object.
168       */
169      public Builder readTransform(BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> value) {
170         readTransform = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
171         return this;
172      }
173
174      /**
175       * Installs the write-side value transform for this property.
176       *
177       * <p>
178       * Applied to the incoming value by {@link BeanPropertyMeta#set(BeanMap,String,Object)} before the raw setter is
179       * invoked.  Defaults to identity (raw value passes through unchanged).
180       *
181       * <p>
182       * Used by the marshalling layer to install {@code ObjectSwap}-aware behavior at bean-meta construction time;
183       * the bean-modeling layer itself only invokes the function and does not directly reference
184       * {@code ObjectSwap}.
185       *
186       * @param value The transform function.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
187       * @return This object.
188       */
189      public Builder writeTransform(BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> value) {
190         writeTransform = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
191         return this;
192      }
193
194      /**
195       * Sets the overridden value of this bean property.
196       *
197       * @param value The overridden value of this bean property.
198       * @return This object.
199       */
200      public Builder overrideValue(Object value) {
201         overrideValue = value;
202         return this;
203      }
204
205      /**
206       * Sets the raw metadata type for this bean property.
207       *
208       * @param value The raw metadata type for this bean property.
209       * @return This object.
210       */
211      public Builder rawMetaType(BeanInfo<?> value) {
212         rawTypeMeta = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
213         typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
214         return this;
215      }
216
217      /**
218       * Sets the raw metadata type for this bean property from a {@link Class}.
219       *
220       * <p>
221       * Convenience overload that resolves the supplied class via the property's
222       * {@link BeanTypeResolver}, allowing callers from the bean-modeling layer to seed the type without
223       * holding a {@link BeanInfo} reference.
224       *
225       * <p>
226       * When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via the commons-side path
227       * ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}), no resolver is available, so
228       * {@code rawTypeMeta}/{@code typeMeta} are left <jk>null</jk> and the property runs in raw-reflection mode.
229       *
230       * @param value The raw metadata type for this bean property.
231       * @return This object.
232       */
233      public Builder rawMetaType(Class<?> value) {
234         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
235         if (bc == null)
236            return this;
237         rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(null, info(value), null);
238         typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
239         return this;
240      }
241
242      /**
243       * Marks this property as readable.
244       *
245       * @return This object.
246       */
247      public Builder canRead() {
248         canRead = true;
249         return this;
250      }
251
252      /**
253       * Marks this property as writable.
254       *
255       * @return This object.
256       */
257      public Builder canWrite() {
258         canWrite = true;
259         return this;
260      }
261
262      /**
263       * Marks this property as a constructor argument.
264       *
265       * @return This object.
266       */
267      public Builder setAsConstructorArg() {
268         isConstructorArg = true;
269         return this;
270      }
271
272      /**
273       * Sets the extra keys method for this bean property.
274       *
275       * @param value The method info that returns extra keys for this property.
276       * @return This object.
277       */
278      public Builder setExtraKeys(MethodInfo value) {
279         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
280         extraKeys = value.accessible();
281         return this;
282      }
283
284      /**
285       * Sets the field for this bean property.
286       *
287       * @param value The field info for this bean property.
288       * @return This object.
289       */
290      public Builder setField(FieldInfo value) {
291         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
292         field = value.accessible();
293         innerField = field;
294         return this;
295      }
296
297      /**
298       * Sets the getter method for this bean property.
299       *
300       * @param value The getter method info for this bean property.
301       * @return This object.
302       */
303      public Builder setGetter(MethodInfo value) {
304         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
305         getter = value.accessible();
306         return this;
307      }
308
309      /**
310       * Sets the inner field for this bean property from a {@link FieldInfo}.
311       *
312       * @param value The field info containing the inner field.
313       * @return This object.
314       */
315      public Builder setInnerField(FieldInfo value) {
316         innerField = assertArgNotNull(ARG_innerField, value);
317         return this;
318      }
319
320      /**
321       * Sets the setter method for this bean property.
322       *
323       * @param value The setter method info for this bean property.
324       * @return This object.
325       */
326      public Builder setSetter(MethodInfo value) {
327         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
328         setter = value.accessible();
329         return this;
330      }
331
332      /**
333       * Validates this bean property configuration.
334       *
335       * <p>
336       * After validation succeeds, marshalling-side callers (currently {@link BeanMeta}) read the
337       * {@link #dictionaryClasses} field to construct the property-level registry lookup and store it in a
338       * side-map keyed by the built {@link BeanPropertyMeta}.  The bean-modeling layer itself no longer carries a
339       * concrete registry reference.
340       *
341       * <p>
342       * When {@code bc} is <jk>null</jk> (commons-side path), this method runs in raw-reflection mode:
343       * annotation reads are routed through {@link BeanConfigContext#getAnnotationProvider()} but no
344       * {@code ClassMeta} resolution or {@code ObjectSwap} discovery is performed; the property's
345       * {@code rawTypeMeta}/{@code typeMeta} stay <jk>null</jk> and the resulting {@link BeanPropertyMeta}
346       * exposes raw getter/setter invocation only.
347       *
348       * @param bc The bean context.  May be <jk>null</jk> for the commons-side path.
349       * @param typeVarImpls Type variable implementations.  Ignored when {@code bc} is <jk>null</jk>.
350       * @param bpro Bean properties read-only set.
351       * @param bpwo Bean properties write-only set.
352       * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property is valid, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
353       * @throws Exception If validation fails.
354       */
355      @SuppressWarnings({
356         "java:S3776", // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
357         "java:S112",  // Generic exception thrown; acceptable for framework/lifecycle methods
358         "java:S6541"  // Brain Method: validate() intentionally consolidates property metadata resolution
359      })
360      public boolean validate(BeanTypeResolver bc, TypeVariables typeVarImpls, Set<String> bpro, Set<String> bpwo) throws Exception {
361
362         var ap = nn(bc) ? bc.getAnnotationProvider() : config.getAnnotationProvider();
363
364         if (field == null && getter == null && setter == null)
365            return false;
366
367         // Settings reads route through BeanConfigContext so the commons-side path works without a MarshallingContext.
368         if (field == null && setter == null && config.isBeansRequireSettersForGetters() && ! isConstructorArg)
369            return false;
370
371         canRead |= (nn(field) || nn(getter));
372         canWrite |= (nn(field) || nn(setter) || isConstructorArg);
373
374         var ifi = innerField;
375         var gi = getter;
376         var si = setter;
377
378         // @MarshalledProp / @Swap annotation reads have been lifted out to the marshalling-side post-processor
379         // (see {@link org.apache.juneau.MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor#process}).  They are processed by {@link BeanMeta}
380         // after this method returns.  The post-processor mutates {@link #swap}, {@link #properties},
381         // {@link #dictionaryClasses}, and {@link #typeMeta} (when a swap is detected) on this builder.
382         dictionaryClasses = emptyList();
383
384         if (nn(innerField)) {
385            var lbp = ap.find(BeanProp.class, ifi);
386            if (nn(bc) && (nn(field) || ine(lbp))) {
387               // Only use field type if it's a bean property or has @BeanProp annotation.
388               // Otherwise, we want to infer the type from the getter or setter.
389               rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(o(last(lbp)).orElse(null), innerField.getFieldType(), typeVarImpls);
390               isUri |= (rawTypeMeta.isUri());
391            }
392            lbp.forEach(x -> {
393               var beanp = x.inner();
394               if (ine(beanp.ro()))
395                  readOnly = b(beanp.ro());
396               if (ine(beanp.wo()))
397                  writeOnly = b(beanp.wo());
398            });
399         }
400
401         if (nn(getter)) {
402            var lbp = ap.find(BeanProp.class, gi);
403            if (nn(bc) && rawTypeMeta == null)
404               rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(o(last(lbp)).orElse(null), getter.getReturnType(), typeVarImpls);
405            if (nn(rawTypeMeta))
406               isUri |= rawTypeMeta.isUri();
407            lbp.forEach(x -> {
408               var beanp = x.inner();
409               if (ine(beanp.ro()))
410                  readOnly = b(beanp.ro());
411               if (ine(beanp.wo()))
412                  writeOnly = b(beanp.wo());
413            });
414         }
415
416         if (nn(setter)) {
417            var lbp = ap.find(BeanProp.class, si);
418            if (nn(bc) && rawTypeMeta == null)
419               rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(o(last(lbp)).orElse(null), setter.getParameterTypes().get(0), typeVarImpls);
420            if (nn(rawTypeMeta))
421               isUri |= rawTypeMeta.isUri();
422            lbp.forEach(x -> {
423               var beanp = x.inner();
424               if (ine(beanp.ro()))
425                  readOnly = b(beanp.ro());
426               if (ine(beanp.wo()))
427                  writeOnly = b(beanp.wo());
428            });
429         }
430
431         // On the commons-side path (bc == null), rawTypeMeta stays null and validate() accepts the property
432         // in raw-reflection mode.  The marshalling-side path still requires a resolvable type.
433         if (nn(bc) && rawTypeMeta == null)
434            return false;
435
436         isDyna = "*".equals(name);
437
438         // Do some annotation validation.  Type-aware validation requires rawTypeMeta — on the commons-side
439         // path we skip it entirely.
440         if (nn(rawTypeMeta)) {
441            var ci = rawTypeMeta;
442            if (nn(getter)) {
443               var pt = getter.getParameterTypes();
444               if (isDyna) {
445                  if (ci.isAssignableTo(Map.class) && ie(pt)) {
446                     isDynaGetterMap = true;
447                  } else if (pt.size() == 1 && pt.get(0).is(String.class)) {
448                     // OK.
449                  } else {
450                     return false;
451                  }
452               } else {
453                  if (! ci.isAssignableTo(getter.getReturnType()))
454                     return false;
455               }
456            }
457            if (nn(setter)) {
458               var pt = setter.getParameterTypes();
459               if (isDyna) {
460                  if (pt.size() == 2 && pt.get(0).is(String.class)) {
461                     // OK.
462                  } else {
463                     return false;
464                  }
465               } else {
466                  if (pt.size() != 1 || ! ci.isAssignableTo(pt.get(0).inner()))
467                     return false;
468               }
469            }
470            if (nn(field)) {
471               if (isDyna) {
472                  if (! field.getFieldType().isAssignableTo(Map.class))
473                     return false;
474               } else {
475                  if (! ci.isAssignableTo(field.getFieldType()))
476                     return false;
477               }
478            }
479
480            if (isDyna) {
481               rawTypeMeta = rawTypeMeta.getValueType();
482               if (rawTypeMeta == null && nn(bc))
483                  rawTypeMeta = bc.objectType();
484            }
485
486            if (typeMeta == null) {
487               if (rawTypeMeta == null && nn(bc))
488                  typeMeta = bc.objectType();
489               else
490                  typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
491            }
492            if (typeMeta == null)
493               typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
494         }
495
496         if (bpro.contains(name) || bpro.contains("*"))
497            readOnly = true;
498         if (bpwo.contains(name) || bpwo.contains("*"))
499            writeOnly = true;
500
501         return true;
502      }
503
504   }
505
506   /**
507    * Creates a builder for {@link #BeanPropertyMeta} objects.
508    *
509    * @param beanMeta The metadata on the bean
510    * @param name The bean property name.
511    * @return A new builder.
512    */
513   public static Builder builder(BeanMeta<?> beanMeta, String name) {
514      return new Builder(beanMeta, name);
515   }
516
517   private final AnnotationProvider ap;                             // Annotation provider for finding annotations on this property.  Sourced from bc (marshalling-side) or beanMeta.getConfig() (commons-side).
518   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<?>>> annotations;     // Memoized list of all annotations on this property.
519   private final BeanTypeResolver bc;                               // The bean-modeling SPI seam to the marshalling-side type resolver.  Null when the owning BeanMeta was built via the commons-side path.
520   private final BeanConfigContext config;                          // Bean-modeling settings facade — always non-null.  Mirrors the BeanMeta's config.
521   private final BeanMeta<?> beanMeta;                              // The bean that this property belongs to.
522   private final boolean canRead;                                   // True if this property can be read.
523   private final boolean canWrite;                                  // True if this property can be written.
524   private final BeanPropertyMeta delegateFor;                      // The bean property that this meta is a delegate for.
525   private final MethodInfo extraKeys;                              // The bean property extraKeys method.
526   private final FieldInfo field;                                   // The bean property field (if it has one).
527   private final MethodInfo getter;                                 // The bean property getter.
528   private final int hashCode;                                      // Cached hash code for this property meta.
529   private final FieldInfo innerField;                              // The bean property field even if private (if it has one).
530   private final boolean isDyna;                                    // True if this is a dyna property (i.e. name="*").
531   private final boolean isDynaGetterMap;                           // True if this is a dyna property where the getter returns a Map directly.
532   private final boolean isUri;                                     // True if this is a URL/URI or annotated with @URI.
533   private final String name;                                       // The name of the property.
534   private final Object overrideValue;                              // The bean property value (if it's an overridden delegate).
535   private final BeanInfo<?> rawTypeMeta;                           // The real class type of the bean property.  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta}; typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam for the eventual move to commons.bean.
536   private final BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> readTransform;  // Applied to raw getter result; identity by default.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam.
537   private final boolean readOnly;                                  // True if this property is read-only.
538   private final MethodInfo setter;                                 // The bean property setter.
539   private final BeanInfo<?> typeMeta;                              // The transformed class type of the bean property.  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta}; typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam.
540   private final BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> writeTransform; // Applied to incoming value before raw setter; identity by default.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam.
541   private final boolean writeOnly;                                 // True if this property is write-only.
542   private final Set<String> views;                                 // Named views this property belongs to; null = untagged (included in all views when defaultViewInclusion is enabled).
543
544   /**
545    * Creates a new BeanPropertyMeta using the contents of the specified builder.
546    *
547    * @param b The builder to copy fields from.
548    */
549   protected BeanPropertyMeta(Builder b) {
550      annotations = memoize(this::findAnnotations);
551      bc = b.bc;
552      config = b.config;
553      beanMeta = b.beanMeta;
554      canRead = b.canRead;
555      canWrite = b.canWrite;
556      delegateFor = b.delegateFor;
557      extraKeys = b.extraKeys;
558      field = b.field;
559      getter = b.getter;
560      innerField = b.innerField;
561      isDyna = b.isDyna;
562      isDynaGetterMap = b.isDynaGetterMap;
563      isUri = b.isUri;
564      name = b.name;
565      overrideValue = b.overrideValue;
566      rawTypeMeta = b.rawTypeMeta;
567      readOnly = b.readOnly;
568      setter = b.setter;
569      typeMeta = b.typeMeta;
570      writeOnly = b.writeOnly;
571      views = b.views != null ? Collections.unmodifiableSet(b.views) : null;
572      readTransform = b.readTransform != null ? b.readTransform : (session, o) -> o;
573      writeTransform = b.writeTransform != null ? b.writeTransform : (session, o) -> o;
574
575      ap = nn(bc) ? bc.getAnnotationProvider() : b.config.getAnnotationProvider();
576      hashCode = h(beanMeta, name);
577   }
578
579   /**
580    * Adds a value to a {@link Collection} or array property.
581    *
582    * <p>
583    * Note that adding values to an array property is inefficient for large arrays since it must copy the array into a
584    * larger array on each operation.
585    *
586    * <p>
587    * <b>Marshalling-only path.</b>  Requires a property built via the marshalling-side construction path
588    * (i.e. with a non-null {@link #getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta} and a non-null backing
589    * {@code MarshallingSession} on the supplied {@link BeanMap}).  When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via
590    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}, this method throws
591    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} because adding into a Collection/array property requires
592    * type-aware element conversion (the marshalling session's {@code convertToType}) that is not available in the
593    * bean-modeling-only path.
594    *
595    * @param m The bean of the field being set.
596    * @param pName
597    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
598    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
599    * @param value The value to add to the field.
600    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If field is not a collection or array.
601    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this property was built via the bean-modeling-only path
602    *    ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).
603    */
604   @SuppressWarnings({
605      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for property add operation with various collection types
606   })
607   public void add(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
608
609      if (rawTypeMeta == null)
610         throw uoex("Property ''{0}'' was built via the bean-modeling-only path; Collection/array add operations require a marshalling context.", name);
611
612      // Read-only beans get their properties stored in a cache.
613      if (m.bean == null) {
614         if (! m.propertyCache.containsKey(name))
615            m.propertyCache.put(name, new ArrayList<>());
616         ((List)m.propertyCache.get(name)).add(value);
617         return;
618      }
619
620      var session = m.getBeanSession();
621
622      var isCollection = rawTypeMeta.isCollection();
623      var isArray = rawTypeMeta.isArray();
624
625      if (! (isCollection || isArray))
626         throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Attempt to add element to property ''{0}'' which is not a collection or array", name);
627
628      var bean = m.getBean(true);
629
630      var elementType = rawTypeMeta.getElementType();
631
632      try {
633         var v = session.convertToType(value, elementType);
634
635         if (isCollection) {
636            Collection c = (Collection)invokeGetter(bean, pName);
637
638            Collection c2 = null;
639            if (nn(c)) {
640               if (canAddTo(c)) {
641                  c.add(v);
642                  return;
643               }
644               c2 = c;
645            }
646
647            if (rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance())
648               c = (Collection)rawTypeMeta.newInstance();
649            else
650               c = new ArrayList<>();
651
652            if (c2 != null)
653               c.addAll(c2);
654
655            c.add(v);
656
657            invokeSetter(bean, pName, c);
658
659         } else /* isArray() */ {
660
661            if (m.arrayPropertyCache == null)
662               m.arrayPropertyCache = new TreeMap<>();
663
664            List l = m.arrayPropertyCache.get(name);
665            if (l == null) {
666               l = new LinkedList();  // ArrayLists and LinkLists appear to perform equally.
667               m.arrayPropertyCache.put(name, l);
668
669               // Copy any existing array values into the temporary list.
670               var oldArray = invokeGetter(bean, pName);
671               copyArrayToList(oldArray, l);
672            }
673
674            // Add new entry to our array.
675            l.add(v);
676         }
677
678      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
679         throw e;
680      } catch (Exception e) {
681         throw brex(e);
682      }
683   }
684
685   /**
686    * Adds a value to a {@link Map} or bean property.
687    *
688    * <p>
689    * <b>Marshalling-only path.</b>  Requires a property built via the marshalling-side construction path
690    * (i.e. with a non-null {@link #getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta} and a non-null backing
691    * {@code MarshallingSession} on the supplied {@link BeanMap}).  When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via
692    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}, this method throws
693    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} because adding into a Map/bean property requires type-aware
694    * value conversion (the marshalling session's {@code convertToType} / {@code toBeanMap}) that is not available
695    * in the bean-modeling-only path.
696    *
697    * @param m The bean of the field being set.
698    * @param pName
699    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
700    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
701    * @param key The key to add to the field.
702    * @param value The value to add to the field.
703    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If field is not a map or array.
704    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this property was built via the bean-modeling-only path
705    *    ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).
706    */
707   @SuppressWarnings({
708      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for property add operation with map key handling
709   })
710   public void add(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, String key, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
711
712      if (rawTypeMeta == null)
713         throw uoex("Property ''{0}'' was built via the bean-modeling-only path; Map/bean add operations require a marshalling context.", name);
714
715      // Read-only beans get their properties stored in a cache.
716      if (m.bean == null) {
717         if (! m.propertyCache.containsKey(name))
718            m.propertyCache.put(name, new LinkedHashMap<>());
719         ((Map)m.propertyCache.get(name)).put(key, value);
720         return;
721      }
722
723      var session = m.getBeanSession();
724
725      var isMap = rawTypeMeta.isMap();
726      var isBean = rawTypeMeta.isBean();
727
728      if (! (isBean || isMap))
729         throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Attempt to add key/value to property ''{0}'' which is not a map or bean", name);
730
731      var bean = m.getBean(true);
732
733      try {
734         var v = session.convertToType(value, rawTypeMeta.getElementType());
735
736         if (isMap) {
737            var map = (Map)invokeGetter(bean, pName);
738
739            if (nn(map)) {
740               map.put(key, v);
741               return;
742            }
743
744            if (rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance())
745               map = (Map)rawTypeMeta.newInstance();
746            else
747               map = new LinkedHashMap<>();
748
749            map.put(key, v);
750
751            invokeSetter(bean, pName, map);
752
753         } else /* isBean() */ {
754
755            var b = invokeGetter(bean, pName);
756
757            if (nn(b)) {
758               ((Map) session.toBeanMap(b)).put(key, v);
759               return;
760            }
761
762            if (rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance(m.getBean(false))) {
763               b = rawTypeMeta.newInstance();
764               ((Map) session.toBeanMap(b)).put(key, v);
765            }
766
767            invokeSetter(bean, pName, b);
768         }
769
770      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
771         throw e;
772      } catch (Exception e) {
773         throw brex(e);
774      }
775   }
776
777   /**
778    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property can be read.
779    *
780    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property can be read.
781    */
782   public boolean canRead() {
783      return canRead;
784   }
785
786   /**
787    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property can be written.
788    *
789    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property can be written.
790    */
791   public boolean canWrite() {
792      return canWrite;
793   }
794
795   @Override /* Overridden from Comparable */
796   public int compareTo(BeanPropertyMeta o) {
797      return cmp(name, o.name);
798   }
799
800   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
801   public boolean equals(Object o) {
802      return (o instanceof BeanPropertyMeta o2) && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.name, y.name) && eq(x.beanMeta, y.beanMeta));
803   }
804
805   /**
806    * Returns all annotations on this property (field, getter, and setter).
807    *
808    * <p>
809    * The annotations are found on:
810    * <ul>
811    *    <li>The field (if present)
812    *    <li>The getter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
813    *    <li>The setter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
814    * </ul>
815    *
816    * <p>
817    * The result is memoized and computed only once.
818    *
819    * @return A list of all annotation infos on this property. Never <jk>null</jk>.
820    */
821   public List<AnnotationInfo> getAnnotations() {
822      return (List)annotations.get();
823   }
824
825   /**
826    * Returns a stream of annotations of the specified type on this property.
827    *
828    * <p>
829    * The annotations are found on:
830    * <ul>
831    *    <li>The field (if present)
832    *    <li>The getter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
833    *    <li>The setter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
834    * </ul>
835    *
836    * @param <A> The annotation type to find.
837    * @param a The annotation class to find.
838    * @return A stream of annotation infos of the specified type. Never <jk>null</jk>.
839    */
840   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> a) {
841      if (a == null)
842         return Stream.empty();
843      return annotations.get().stream()
844         .filter(x -> x.isType(a))
845         .map(x -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)x);
846   }
847
848   /**
849    * Helper method to find all annotations on this property.
850    *
851    * @return A list of all annotation infos found on the field, getter, and setter.
852    */
853   private List<AnnotationInfo<?>> findAnnotations() {
854      var result = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<?>>();
855      if (nn(field))
856         result.addAll(ap.find(field));
857      if (nn(getter))
858         result.addAll(ap.find(getter, SELF, MATCHING_METHODS, RETURN_TYPE, PACKAGE));
859      if (nn(setter))
860         result.addAll(ap.find(setter, SELF, MATCHING_METHODS, RETURN_TYPE, PACKAGE));
861      if (nn(extraKeys))
862         result.addAll(ap.find(extraKeys, SELF, MATCHING_METHODS, RETURN_TYPE, PACKAGE));
863      return u(result);
864   }
865
866   /**
867    * Equivalent to calling {@link BeanMap#get(Object)}, but is faster since it avoids looking up the property meta.
868    *
869    * @param m The bean map to get the transformed value from.
870    * @param pName
871    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
872    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
873    * @return
874    *    The property value.
875    *    <br>Returns <jk>null</jk> if this is a write-only property.
876    */
877   public Object get(BeanMap<?> m, String pName) {
878      return m.meta.onReadProperty(m.bean, pName, getInner(m, pName));
879   }
880
881   /**
882    * Returns the bean meta that this property belongs to.
883    *
884    * @return The bean meta that this property belongs to.
885    */
886   public BeanMeta<?> getBeanMeta() { return beanMeta; }
887
888   /**
889    * Returns the bean dictionary in use for this bean property.
890    *
891    * <p>
892    * The order of lookup for the dictionary is as follows:
893    * <ol>
894    *    <li>Dictionary defined via {@code @MarshalledProp(dictionary)}.
895    *    <li>Dictionary defined via {@code MarshallingContext.Builder<?>#beanDictionary(Class...)}.
896    * </ol>
897    *
898    * <p>
899    * The per-property registry no longer lives on this object; it is stored in a marshalling-side
900    * side-map on {@link BeanMeta} keyed by {@link BeanPropertyMeta}.  This method delegates to
901    * {@link BeanMeta#getPropertyBeanRegistry(BeanPropertyMeta)} for backwards compatibility with existing call sites
902    * in the marshalling layer (parser/serializer sessions, XML content-property handling, etc.).
903    *
904    * <p>
905    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanRegistryLookup}).  Marshalling-side callers that need
906    * implementation-specific behavior cast at the call site.
907    *
908    * @return The bean dictionary in use for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if no registry was registered.
909    */
910   public BeanRegistryLookup getBeanRegistry() { return beanMeta.getPropertyBeanRegistry(this); }
911
912   /**
913    * Returns the {@link BeanInfo} of the class of this property.
914    *
915    * <p>
916    * If this property or the property type class has a {@code ObjectSwap} associated with it, this method returns the
917    * transformed class meta.
918    * This matches the class type that is used by the {@link #get(BeanMap,String)} and
919    * {@link #set(BeanMap,String,Object)} methods.
920    *
921    * <p>
922    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanInfo}).  Marshalling-side callers that need the
923    * {@code ClassMeta} narrowing must cast — the concrete instance in-tree is always a {@code ClassMeta}.
924    *
925    * @return The {@link BeanInfo} of the class of this property, or <jk>null</jk> if this property was built via
926    *    the bean-modeling-only path.
927    */
928   public BeanInfo<?> getBeanInfo() { return typeMeta; }
929
930   /**
931    * Returns the metadata on the property that this metadata is a delegate for.
932    *
933    * @return the metadata on the property that this metadata is a delegate for, or this object if it's not a delegate.
934    */
935   public BeanPropertyMeta getDelegateFor() { return coalesce(delegateFor, this); }
936
937   /**
938    * Returns the {@link Map} object returned by the DynaBean getter.
939    *
940    * <p>
941    * The DynaBean property is the property whose name is <js>"*"</js> and returns a map of "extra" properties on the
942    * bean.
943    *
944    * @param bean The bean.
945    * @return
946    *    The map returned by the getter, or an empty map if the getter returned <jk>null</jk> or this isn't a DynaBean
947    *    property.
948    * @throws IllegalArgumentException Thrown by method invocation.
949    * @throws IllegalAccessException Thrown by method invocation.
950    * @throws InvocationTargetException Thrown by method invocation.
951    */
952   public Map<String,Object> getDynaMap(Object bean) throws IllegalArgumentException, IllegalAccessException, InvocationTargetException {
953      if (isDyna) {
954         if (nn(extraKeys) && nn(getter) && ! isDynaGetterMap) {
955            Map<String,Object> m = map();
956            ((Collection<String>)extraKeys.invoke(bean)).forEach(x -> safe(() -> m.put(x, getter.invoke(bean, x))));
957            return m;
958         }
959         if (nn(getter) && isDynaGetterMap)
960            return (Map)getter.invoke(bean);
961         if (nn(field))
962            return (Map)field.get(bean);
963         throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined, name);
964      }
965      return emptyMap();
966   }
967
968   /**
969    * Returns the field for this property.
970    *
971    * @return The field info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no field associated with this bean property.
972    */
973   public FieldInfo getField() { return field; }
974
975   /**
976    * Returns the getter method for this property.
977    *
978    * @return The getter method info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no getter method.
979    */
980   public MethodInfo getGetter() { return getter; }
981
982   /**
983    * Returns the field for this property even if the field is private.
984    *
985    * @return The field info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no field associated with this bean property.
986    */
987   public FieldInfo getInnerField() { return innerField; }
988
989   /**
990    * Returns the name of this bean property.
991    *
992    * @return The name of the bean property.
993    */
994   public String getName() { return name; }
995
996   /**
997    * Equivalent to calling {@link BeanMap#getRaw(Object)}, but is faster since it avoids looking up the property meta.
998    *
999    * @param m The bean map to get the transformed value from.
1000    * @param pName
1001    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
1002    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
1003    * @return The raw property value.
1004    */
1005   public Object getRaw(BeanMap<?> m, String pName) {
1006      try {
1007         // Read-only beans have their properties stored in a cache until getBean() is called.
1008         var bean = m.bean;
1009         if (bean == null)
1010            return m.propertyCache.get(name);
1011
1012      return invokeGetter(bean, pName);
1013
1014   } catch (Exception e) {
1015      if (config.isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()) {
1016         if (nn(rawTypeMeta) && rawTypeMeta.isPrimitive())
1017            return rawTypeMeta.getPrimitiveDefault();
1018         return null;
1019      }
1020      throw brex(e, beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Exception occurred while getting property ''{0}''", name);
1021   }
1022   }
1023
1024   /**
1025    * Returns the setter method for this property.
1026    *
1027    * @return The setter method info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no setter method.
1028    */
1029   public MethodInfo getSetter() { return setter; }
1030
1031   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
1032   public int hashCode() {
1033      return hashCode;
1034   }
1035
1036   /**
1037    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is named <js>"*"</js>.
1038    *
1039    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is named <js>"*"</js>.
1040    */
1041   public boolean isDyna() { return isDyna; }
1042
1043   /**
1044    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property is read-only.
1045    *
1046    * <p>
1047    * This implies the property MIGHT be writable, but that parsers should not set a value for it.
1048    *
1049    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property is read-only.
1050    */
1051   public boolean isReadOnly() { return readOnly; }
1052
1053   /**
1054    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is a URI.
1055    *
1056    * <p>
1057    * A bean property can be considered a URI if any of the following are true:
1058    * <ul>
1059    *    <li>Property class type is {@link URL} or {@link URI}.
1060    *    <li>Property class type is annotated with {@code @Uri}.
1061    *    <li>Property getter, setter, or field is annotated with {@code @Uri}.
1062    * </ul>
1063    *
1064    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is a URI.
1065    */
1066   public boolean isUri() { return isUri; }
1067
1068   /**
1069    * Returns the set of named views this property belongs to, or <jk>null</jk> if the property is untagged.
1070    *
1071    * <p>
1072    * An untagged property (one with no {@code @MarshalledProp(view=...)} annotation) follows the
1073    * default-view-inclusion policy: it is included under every active view when the policy is enabled
1074    * (the default behavior), or excluded from all views when the policy is disabled.
1075    *
1076    * <p>
1077    * A tagged property is included only when the active view name is contained in this set.
1078    *
1079    * @return The set of named views, or <jk>null</jk> if no view membership was declared.
1080    */
1081   public Set<String> getViews() { return views; }
1082
1083   /**
1084    * Equivalent to calling {@link BeanMap#put(String, Object)}, but is faster since it avoids looking up the property
1085    * meta.
1086    *
1087    * <p>
1088    * This is a no-op on a read-only property.
1089    *
1090    * @param m The bean map to set the property value on.
1091    * @param pName
1092    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
1093    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
1094    * @param value The value to set.
1095    * @return The previous property value.
1096    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If property could not be set.
1097    */
1098   @SuppressWarnings({
1099      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
1100      "java:S6541", // Session objects are single-threaded by design
1101   })
1102   public Object set(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
1103      Object value1 = m.meta.onWriteProperty(m.bean, pName, value);
1104      try {
1105
1106         if (readOnly)
1107            return null;
1108
1109         var session = m.getBeanSession();
1110
1111         // Apply the install-time write transform (identity by default; swap-aware in the marshalling layer).
1112         value1 = writeTransform.apply(session, value1);
1113
1114         // Generic SPI hook: a transform that returns SKIP_VALUE causes the set to be skipped entirely so the
1115         // bean's pre-existing value (typically a field-initializer) is preserved.  Used by the marshalling
1116         // layer's per-property null-coercion SKIP mode.
1117         if (value1 == SKIP_VALUE)
1118            return null;
1119
1120         if (m.bean == null) {
1121
1122            // Read-only beans get their properties stored in a cache.
1123            if (nn(m.propertyCache))
1124               return m.propertyCache.put(name, value1);
1125
1126            throw brex("Non-existent bean instance on bean.");
1127         }
1128
1129         // Raw-reflection path: when the owning BeanMeta was built via the commons-side path, rawTypeMeta and
1130         // bc are null and there is no marshalling-aware type conversion to perform.  Just invoke the setter
1131         // (or write the field) directly.
1132         if (rawTypeMeta == null) {
1133            var bean = m.getBean(true);
1134            var old = (nn(getter) || nn(field)) ? get(m, pName) : null;
1135            invokeSetter(bean, pName, value1);
1136            return old;
1137         }
1138
1139         var isMap = rawTypeMeta.isMap();
1140         var isCollection = rawTypeMeta.isCollection();
1141
1142         if ((! isDyna) && field == null && setter == null && ! (isMap || isCollection)) {
1143            if ((value1 == null && config.isIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()) || config.isIgnoreMissingSetters())
1144               return null;
1145            throw brex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Setter or public field not defined on property ''{0}''", name);
1146         }
1147
1148         var bean = m.getBean(true);  // Don't use getBean() because it triggers array creation!
1149
1150         return setPropertyValue(m, pName, value1, bean, isMap, isCollection, session);
1151      } catch (org.apache.juneau.commons.BasicRuntimeException e2) {
1152         // Preserve previous behavior: marshalling-side BasicRuntimeException (notably ParseException raised by
1153         // session.parseToMap/parseToList or writeTransform) gets wrapped in BeanRuntimeException so callers can
1154         // uniformly catch BeanRuntimeException.  BeanRuntimeException itself does not extend BasicRuntimeException
1155         // so it propagates unchanged.
1156         throw brex(e2);
1157      }
1158   }
1159
1160   @SuppressWarnings({
1161      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for complex property setter
1162      "java:S6541" // Brain method acceptable - complex property value setting logic requires high LOC/complexity
1163   })
1164   private Object setPropertyValue(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, Object value1, Object bean, boolean isMap, boolean isCollection, BeanSession session) {
1165      try {
1166         var r = (config.isBeanMapPutReturnsOldValue() || isMap || isCollection) && (nn(getter) || nn(field)) ? get(m, pName) : null;
1167         var propertyClass = rawTypeMeta.inner();
1168         var pcInfo = rawTypeMeta;
1169
1170         if (value1 == null && (isMap || isCollection)) {
1171            invokeSetter(bean, pName, null);
1172            return r;
1173         }
1174
1175         var vc = value1 == null ? null : value1.getClass();
1176
1177         if (isMap && (setter == null || ! pcInfo.isAssignableFrom(vc))) {
1178
1179            if (! (value1 instanceof Map)) {
1180               if (value1 instanceof CharSequence value21)
1181                  value1 = session.parseToMap(value21);
1182               else
1183                  throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}''", name, propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1184            }
1185
1186            var valueMap = (Map)value1;
1187            var propMap = (Map)r;
1188            var keyType = rawTypeMeta.getKeyType();
1189            var valueType = rawTypeMeta.getValueType();
1190
1191            // If the property type is abstract, then we either need to reuse the existing
1192            // map (if it's not null), or try to assign the value directly.
1193            if (! rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance()) {
1194               if (propMap == null) {
1195                  if (setter == null && field == null)
1196                     throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(),
1197                        "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter or public field is defined, and the current value is null", name,
1198                        propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1199
1200                  if (propertyClass.isInstance(valueMap)) {
1201                     if (! (keyType.isObject() && valueType.isObject())) {
1202                        var needsConversion = Flag.create();
1203                        valueMap.forEach((k, v2) -> {
1204                           if (nn(k) && ! keyType.isObject() && ! keyType.isInstance(k))
1205                              needsConversion.set();
1206                           if (nn(v2) && ! valueType.isInstance(v2)) {
1207                              needsConversion.set();
1208                           }
1209                        });
1210                        if (needsConversion.isSet())
1211                           valueMap = (Map)session.convertToType(valueMap, rawTypeMeta);
1212                     }
1213                     invokeSetter(bean, pName, valueMap);
1214                     return r;
1215                  }
1216                  throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(),
1217                     "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{2}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because the assigned map cannot be converted to the specified type because the property type is abstract, and the property value is currently null",
1218                     name, propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1219               }
1220            } else {
1221               if (propMap == null) {
1222                  propMap = BeanInstantiator.of(Map.class).type(rawTypeMeta).preferZeroArgConstructor().run();
1223               } else {
1224                  propMap.clear();
1225               }
1226            }
1227
1228            // Set the values.
1229            for (var e : ((Set<Map.Entry>)valueMap.entrySet())) {
1230               var k1 = e.getKey();
1231               var v1 = e.getValue();
1232               if (! keyType.isObject())
1233                  k1 = session.convertToType(k1, keyType);
1234               if (! valueType.isObject())
1235                  v1 = session.convertToType(v1, valueType);
1236               propMap.put(k1, v1);
1237            }
1238            if (nn(setter) || nn(field))
1239               invokeSetter(bean, pName, propMap);
1240
1241         } else if (isCollection && (setter == null || ! pcInfo.isAssignableFrom(vc))) {
1242
1243            if (! (value1 instanceof Collection)) {
1244               if (value1 instanceof CharSequence value2)
1245                  value1 = session.parseToList(value2);
1246               else
1247                  throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}''", name, propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1248            }
1249
1250            var valueList = (Collection)value1;
1251            var propList = (Collection)r;
1252            var elementType = rawTypeMeta.getElementType();
1253
1254            // If the property type is abstract, then we either need to reuse the existing
1255            // collection (if it's not null), or try to assign the value directly.
1256            if (! rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance()) {
1257               if (propList == null) {
1258                  if (setter == null && field == null)
1259                     throw brex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(),
1260                        "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter or public field is defined, and the current value is null", name,
1261                        propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1262
1263                  if (propertyClass.isInstance(valueList) || (nn(setter) && setter.getParameterTypes().get(0).is(Collection.class))) {
1264                     if (! elementType.isObject()) {
1265                        // Element coercion is required.  Build the coerced elements into a concrete collection
1266                        // whose shape is assignable to the (abstract) property type — never a bare ArrayList —
1267                        // so Deque/Queue/SortedSet fields aren't handed an incompatible container.  (The source
1268                        // value can incidentally satisfy propertyClass.isInstance(...) — e.g. the parser's
1269                        // generic list is a LinkedList, which is-a Deque/Queue — yet still need coercion.)
1270                        Collection coerced = createDefaultCollectionForAbstractType(propertyClass);
1271                        for (var v : valueList) {
1272                           var needsConversion = v == null ? elementType.isOptional() : ! elementType.isInstance(v);
1273                           coerced.add(needsConversion ? session.convertToType(v, elementType) : v);
1274                        }
1275                        valueList = coerced;
1276                     }
1277                     invokeSetter(bean, pName, valueList);
1278                     return r;
1279                  }
1280
1281                  // Robust best-effort-then-fallback materialization — never returns null (see helper Javadoc).
1282                  propList = createDefaultCollectionForAbstractType(propertyClass);
1283                  invokeSetter(bean, pName, propList);
1284               }
1285               propList.clear();
1286            } else {
1287               if (propList == null) {
1288                  propList = BeanInstantiator.of(Collection.class).type(rawTypeMeta).preferZeroArgConstructor().run();
1289                  invokeSetter(bean, pName, propList);
1290               } else {
1291                  propList.clear();
1292               }
1293            }
1294
1295            // Set the values.
1296            for (var x : valueList) {
1297               if (! elementType.isObject())
1298                  x = session.convertToType(x, elementType);
1299               propList.add(x);
1300            }
1301
1302         } else {
1303            value1 = session.convertToMemberType(bean, value1, rawTypeMeta);
1304            invokeSetter(bean, pName, value1);
1305         }
1306
1307         return r;
1308
1309      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
1310         throw e;
1311      } catch (Exception e1) {
1312         if (config.isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()) {
1313            if (rawTypeMeta.isPrimitive())
1314               return rawTypeMeta.getPrimitiveDefault();
1315            return null;
1316         }
1317         throw brex(e1, beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Error occurred trying to set property ''{0}''", name);
1318      }
1319   }
1320
1321   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
1322      // @formatter:off
1323      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
1324         .a(PROP_field, field)
1325         .a(PROP_getter, getter)
1326         .a(PROP_name, name)
1327         .a(PROP_setter, setter)
1328         .a(PROP_type, cn(rawTypeMeta));
1329      // @formatter:on
1330   }
1331
1332   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
1333   public String toString() {
1334      return r(properties());
1335   }
1336
1337   private Object getInner(BeanMap<?> m, String pName) {
1338      try {
1339
1340         if (writeOnly)
1341            return null;
1342
1343         if (nn(overrideValue))
1344            return overrideValue;
1345
1346         // Read-only beans have their properties stored in a cache until getBean() is called.
1347         var bean = m.bean;
1348         if (bean == null)
1349            return m.propertyCache.get(name);
1350
1351         var session = m.getBeanSession();
1352         var o = getRaw(m, pName);
1353
1354         return readTransform.apply(session, o);
1355
1356      } catch (Exception e) {
1357         if (config.isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()) {
1358            if (nn(rawTypeMeta) && rawTypeMeta.isPrimitive())
1359               return rawTypeMeta.getPrimitiveDefault();
1360            return null;
1361         }
1362         throw brex(e, beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Exception occurred while getting property ''{0}''", name);
1363      }
1364   }
1365
1366   private Object invokeGetter(Object bean, String pName) throws IllegalArgumentException {
1367      if (isDyna) {
1368         Map m = null;
1369         if (nn(getter)) {
1370            if (! isDynaGetterMap)
1371               return getter.invoke(bean, pName);
1372            m = (Map)getter.invoke(bean);
1373         } else if (nn(field))
1374            m = (Map)field.get(bean);
1375         else
1376            throw brex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined, name);
1377         return (m == null ? null : m.get(pName));
1378      }
1379      if (nn(getter))
1380         return getter.invoke(bean);
1381      if (nn(field))
1382         return field.get(bean);
1383      throw brex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined, name);
1384   }
1385
1386   private Object invokeSetter(Object bean, String pName, Object val) throws IllegalArgumentException {
1387      if (isDyna) {
1388         if (nn(setter))
1389            return setter.invoke(bean, pName, val);
1390         Map m = null;
1391         if (nn(field))
1392            m = (Map<String,Object>)field.get(bean);
1393         else if (nn(getter))
1394            m = (Map<String,Object>)getter.invoke(bean);
1395         else
1396            throw brex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter is defined on this property, and the existing property value is null",
1397               name, classNameForError(), cn(val));
1398         return (m == null ? null : m.put(pName, val));
1399      }
1400      if (nn(setter))
1401         return setter.invoke(bean, val);
1402      if (nn(field)) {
1403         field.set(bean, val);
1404         return null;
1405      }
1406      throw brex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter is defined on this property, and the existing property value is null", name,
1407         classNameForError(), cn(val));
1408   }
1409
1410   private String classNameForError() {
1411      var cm = getBeanInfo();
1412      if (nn(cm))
1413         return cm.getName();
1414      return beanMeta.getClassInfo().getName();
1415   }
1416
1417   /**
1418    * Returns a concrete {@link Collection} instance suitable for populating an abstract collection-typed bean
1419    * property that has no setter and no {@code @BeanProp(type=...)} override.
1420    *
1421    * <p>
1422    * Resolution is <b>best-effort-then-fallback</b>:
1423    * <ol>
1424    *    <li><b>Best-effort materialization of the declared type.</b>  First attempts to instantiate
1425    *       {@code propertyClass} directly via {@link BeanInstantiator} (preferring a zero-arg constructor).  This
1426    *       succeeds when the declared type is itself instantiable — e.g. a concrete {@link AbstractSet} /
1427    *       {@link AbstractList} subclass with an accessible no-arg constructor, or any type
1428    *       {@code BeanInstantiator} can otherwise construct.  The attempt never throws; on any failure it silently
1429    *       degrades to the shape-based fallback below.
1430    *    <li><b>Shape-based fallback.</b>  When the declared type is genuinely not instantiable (a {@link Set} /
1431    *       {@link List} / {@link Queue} interface, a truly abstract class, or a class with no usable constructor),
1432    *       a default concrete type is chosen by collection shape:
1433    *       <ul>
1434    *          <li>{@link SortedSet} / {@link NavigableSet} &rarr; {@link TreeSet}
1435    *          <li>{@link Set} &rarr; {@link LinkedHashSet} (preserves insertion order)
1436    *          <li>{@link Deque} / {@link Queue} &rarr; {@link ArrayDeque}
1437    *          <li>{@link List} / raw {@link Collection} (and any other collection shape) &rarr; {@link ArrayList}
1438    *       </ul>
1439    * </ol>
1440    *
1441    * @param propertyClass The declared (abstract or interface) collection type of the property.
1442    * @return A new, empty, concrete {@link Collection} instance.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1443    */
1444   private Collection<?> createDefaultCollectionForAbstractType(Class<?> propertyClass) {
1445      // Best-effort: try to materialize the declared type itself (handles concrete AbstractSet/AbstractList
1446      // subclasses, registered defaults, etc.).  Never throws — degrades to the shape-based fallback on failure.
1447      Collection declared = BeanInstantiator.of(Collection.class).type(info(propertyClass)).preferZeroArgConstructor().fallback(() -> null).run();
1448      if (nn(declared))
1449         return declared;
1450
1451      // Shape-based fallback for genuinely non-instantiable declared types.
1452      if (SortedSet.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass) || NavigableSet.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass))
1453         return new TreeSet<>();
1454      if (Set.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass))
1455         return new LinkedHashSet<>();
1456      if (Deque.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass) || Queue.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass))
1457         return new ArrayDeque<>();
1458      return new ArrayList<>();  // List or raw Collection (and any other collection shape).
1459   }
1460
1461   /**
1462    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property is write-only.
1463    *
1464    * <p>
1465    * This implies the property MIGHT be readable, but that serializers should not serialize it.
1466    *
1467    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property is write-only.
1468    */
1469   protected boolean isWriteOnly() { return writeOnly; }
1470
1471   /**
1472    * Sets an array field on this bean.
1473    *
1474    * <p>
1475    * Works on both <c>Object</c> and primitive arrays.
1476    *
1477    * <p>
1478    * <b>Marshalling-only path.</b>  Requires a property built via the marshalling-side construction path
1479    * (i.e. with a non-null {@link #getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta}).  When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via
1480    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}, this method throws
1481    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} because the array element type is only known via {@code rawTypeMeta},
1482    * which is not populated in the bean-modeling-only path.
1483    *
1484    * @param bean The bean of the field.
1485    * @param l The collection to use to set the array field.
1486    * @throws IllegalArgumentException Thrown by method invocation.
1487    * @throws IllegalAccessException Thrown by method invocation.
1488    * @throws InvocationTargetException Thrown by method invocation.
1489    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this property was built via the bean-modeling-only path
1490    *    ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).
1491    */
1492   protected void setArray(Object bean, List l) throws IllegalArgumentException, IllegalAccessException, InvocationTargetException {
1493      if (rawTypeMeta == null)
1494         throw uoex("Property ''{0}'' was built via the bean-modeling-only path; setArray requires a marshalling context.", name);
1495      var array = toArray(l, this.rawTypeMeta.getElementType().inner());
1496      invokeSetter(bean, name, array);
1497   }
1498}
